title: carex reuteriana subsp. tartessiana
intro: Carex reuteriana subsp. tartessiana, also known as a type of sedge, is a plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Carex reuteriana subsp. tartessiana include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural expectorant, making it a useful herbal remedy for respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
